draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer CRM copy that feels like talking to a friend, not a enterprise software manual
facade.label: Conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: Voice should use contractions, casual language, occasional humor; messages feel like peer-to-peer advice
facade.rejectImplies: Voice should be formal, precise, corporate; messages maintain professional distance and institutional tone

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer CRM copy that feels like talking to a friend, not a enterprise software manual
evidence.implication: Voice should use contractions, casual language, occasional humor; messages feel like peer-to-peer advice

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer scannable card layouts over dense contact tables for quick visual reference
facade.label: Client list as cards
facade.acceptImplies: Explore card-based UI patterns for client overviews, prioritize visual hierarchy and whitespace
facade.rejectImplies: Explore data-dense table layouts, rows/columns, prioritize information density and quick sorting

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer seeing client or project changes appear immediately without page reload, reducing cognitive load when managing multiple concurrent relationships
facade.axisTargeted: Motion and Behavior — animated vs static, transitions vs instant
facade.label: Instant list updates
facade.acceptImplies: The CRM should prioritize real-time feedback and snappy state changes over graceful loading states or page transitions
facade.rejectImplies: The CRM should use deliberate page loads, refresh buttons, or staged transitions to give users clear breakpoints and time to absorb changes

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer a persistent left sidebar for navigation (clients, projects, invoices) paired with card-based layouts in the main area for scanning multiple projects at a glance.
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward persistent structure + visual hierarchy via cards; likely values quick scanning and context retention.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer tab-based navigation or list-dense layouts; values compact space or sequential workflows.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer low-light, stripped-down UIs that reduce visual noise and feel professional/serious
facade.axisTargeted: Light vs Dark + Complexity (simple vs feature-rich)
facade.label: Minimal dark interface
facade.acceptImplies: User leans toward dark mode, sparse layouts, and understated typography—likely values focus and reduced cognitive load
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer brighter, more colorful, or feature-visible designs—possibly values warmth, approachability, or visual hierarchy through color

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to interfaces that feel handcrafted and natural rather than corporate-sterile, suggesting a preference for organic warmth over digital coldness in their CRM environment.
facade.label: Warm, earthy tones
facade.acceptImplies: The CRM should use warm color palettes (terracottas, ochres, soft browns), natural textures, and a tactile feel that evokes paper, wood, or clay—creating a human-centered rather than machine-centered aesthetic.
facade.rejectImplies: The CRM should lean toward cooler, cleaner aesthetics (blues, grays, whites) with smooth surfaces and a more minimalist, tech-forward appearance that signals efficiency and precision over warmth.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
Whether user wants tools clustered in one sidebar/dashboard vs. spread across tabs and modals—reveals navigation preference for a freelancer juggling multiple clients
Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business-like assistant or a supportive collaborator
Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free workspace vs. energetic, motivational environment—reveals stress tolerance and work style for solo freelancers
Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards with metrics/charts vs. simple lists and summaries—reveals how much visibility into business health they need
Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, deliberate animations—reveals preference for speed-focused vs. thoughtful interface rhythm

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools clustered in one sidebar/dashboard vs. spread across tabs and modals—reveals navigation preference for a freelancer juggling multiple clients
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business-like assistant or a supportive collaborator
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free workspace vs. energetic, motivational environment—reveals stress tolerance and work style for solo freelancers
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards with metrics/charts vs. simple lists and summaries—reveals how much visibility into business health they need
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, deliberate animations—reveals preference for speed-focused vs. thoughtful interface rhythm
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers using a CRM want trustworthy, straightforward communication that respects their time and professionalism—the opposite of forced friendliness.
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and Personality (friendly-casual vs. professional-formal spectrum)
facade.label: All business, zero jokes
facade.acceptImplies: The product voice should prioritize clarity, efficiency, and competence over warmth; use active voice, direct instructions, minimal personality
facade.rejectImplies: Confirms that freelancers prefer approachable, human-centered copy; conversational tone is the right direction
